call_outcome: Customer requested no further calls and preferred communication via WhatsApp or email.
customer_sentiment: Frustrated and irritated due to repeated calls, but calmed down by the advisor's apology and solution.

strengths.title: Apologized and acknowledged customer's frustration
strengths.evidence: "I completely understand why you are upset. I feel frustrated too."

strengths.title: Offered to update communication preferences
strengths.evidence: "If you would like, I can immediately mark your profile so no one else can contact you without your permission."

strengths.title: Offered alternative communication method
strengths.evidence: "Would you prefer if I simply send the program detail by WhatsApp or email? You can go through them whenever you are free."

improvements.title: Improve rapport building
improvements.suggestion: Advisor could have acknowledged the customer's frustration earlier and expressed empathy more proactively to build rapport.

improvements.title: Clarify purpose of call earlier
improvements.suggestion: Advisor should have clearly stated the purpose of the call at the beginning to avoid customer irritation.

improvements.title: Budget qualification missing
improvements.suggestion: Advisor did not attempt to qualify the customer's budget or willingness to invest in the product.

red_flags.statement: "Again, Freightnova. Seriously, how many people are going to call me? I've already told three different people that I'm busy."
red_flags.reason: Customer is frustrated due to multiple calls from the same company, indicating poor internal communication and potential compliance issues.

summary: The advisor handled the call with professionalism and empathy, apologizing for the inconvenience and offering to update the customer's communication preferences. However, the call was reactive rather than proactive, with the advisor failing to clarify the call's purpose early and not qualifying the customer's budget or interest effectively. The customer's frustration stemmed from repeated calls by different agents, highlighting an internal communication issue. Overall, the advisor managed the situation well but could improve in rapport building and needs discovery.
